import Fastify from 'fastify';
import cors from '@fastify/cors';
import jwt from '@fastify/jwt';
import rateLimit from '@fastify/rate-limit';
import multipart from '@fastify/multipart';
import fastifyStatic from '@fastify/static';
import { Server } from 'socket.io';
import dotenv from 'dotenv';
import path from 'path';
import fs from 'fs';
import { scraperService } from './services/scraper';
import { authService } from './services/auth';
import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';
import { scraperQueue, initScraperWorker } from './services/scraperQueue';
import { setSocketInstance } from './services/socketManager';
import { initLiveCommentsHandler } from './services/liveCommentsHandler';
import { initPresenceHandler } from './services/presenceHandler';
import { initNotificationHandler } from './services/notificationHandler';
import { contentRoutes } from './routes/content';
import { userRoutes } from './routes/users';
import { taxonomyRoutes } from './routes/taxonomy';
import { adminRoutes } from './routes/admin';
import { uploadRoutes } from './routes/upload';
import { engagementRoutes } from './routes/engagement';
import { passwordResetRoutes } from './routes/passwordReset';
import { scrapeApiRoutes } from './routes/scrapeApi';
import { permissionRoutes } from './routes/permissions';
import { analyticsRoutes } from './routes/analytics';
import { scraperManagementRoutes } from './routes/scraperManagement';
import { seoRoutes } from './routes/seo';
import { logActivity } from './middleware/activityLogger';
import { trackPageViewMiddleware } from './middleware/analyticsMiddleware';
import { loginSchema, registerSchema } from './schemas/validation';
import { logger, LogLevel } from './services/loggerService';
import { errorHandler } from './middleware/errorHandler';
import { compositeRequestLoggerMiddleware } from './middleware/requestLogger';

dotenv.config();

const prisma = new PrismaClient();
const fastify = Fastify({
  logger: false, // Disable default logger, use our custom logger
});

// Initialize logger
const logLevel = (process.env.LOG_LEVEL as LogLevel) || LogLevel.INFO;
logger.setLogLevel(logLevel);
logger.info('Logger initialized', { logLevel, environment: process.env.NODE_ENV });

// Plugins
fastify.register(cors, {
  origin: process.env.CORS_ORIGIN || '*',
});

fastify.register(jwt, {
  secret: process.env.JWT_SECRET || 'supersecret',
});

// Rate Limiting
fastify.register(rateLimit, {
  max: 100,
  timeWindow: '1 minute',
});

// Multipart (file upload)
fastify.register(multipart, {
  limits: { fileSize: 5 * 1024 * 1024 } // 5MB
});

// Static file serving (uploads)
const uploadsDir = path.join(process.cwd(), 'uploads');
if (!fs.existsSync(uploadsDir)) {
  fs.mkdirSync(uploadsDir, { recursive: true });
}
fastify.register(fastifyStatic, {
  root: uploadsDir,
  prefix: '/uploads/',
  decorateReply: false,
});

// Request logging middleware - must be first
fastify.addHook('onRequest', compositeRequestLoggerMiddleware);

// Analytics middleware - track all page views
fastify.addHook('onRequest', trackPageViewMiddleware);

// Error handler hook - must be last
fastify.setErrorHandler(errorHandler);

// Basic Route
fastify.get('/health', async () => {
  return { status: 'ok', timestamp: new Date().toISOString() };
});

// --- AUTH ROUTES ---

// Register
fastify.post('/api/auth/register', async (request, reply) => {
  const parsed = registerSchema.safeParse(request.body);
  if (!parsed.success) {
    return reply.status(400).send({ success: false, message: parsed.error.errors[0].message });
  }

  const { username, email, password } = parsed.data;

  try {
    const user = await authService.register({ username, email, password });
    const token = fastify.jwt.sign({ id: user.id, role: user.role }, { expiresIn: '7d' });
    return { success: true, data: { user, token } };
  } catch (error: any) {
    const status = error.statusCode || 500;
    return reply.status(status).send({ success: false, message: error.message || 'Gagal mendaftar' });
  }
});

// Login (stricter rate limit)
fastify.post('/api/auth/login', { config: { rateLimit: { max: 10, timeWindow: '1 minute' } } }, async (request, reply) => {
  const parsed = loginSchema.safeParse(request.body);
  if (!parsed.success) {
    return reply.status(400).send({ success: false, message: parsed.error.errors[0].message });
  }

  const { email, password } = parsed.data;

  try {
    const user = await authService.login(email, password);
    const token = fastify.jwt.sign({ id: user.id, role: user.role }, { expiresIn: '7d' });
    await logActivity(request, 'AUTH', 'LOGIN', `User ${user.username} berhasil login`);
    return { success: true, data: { user, token } };
  } catch (error: any) {
    const status = error.statusCode || 500;
    return reply.status(status).send({ success: false, message: error.message || 'Gagal login' });
  }
});

// Get current user profile (protected)
fastify.get('/api/auth/me', async (request, reply) => {
  try {
    await request.jwtVerify();
    const { id } = request.user as { id: string };
    const user = await authService.getProfile(id);
    return { success: true, data: user };
  } catch (error: any) {
    return reply.status(401).send({ success: false, message: 'Token tidak valid atau sudah expired' });
  }
});

// Scraper Route
fastify.get('/api/trending', async (request, reply) => {
  const trending = await scraperService.scrapeTrending();
  return { success: true, data: trending };
});

// Content Routes (Anime, Manga, Donghua) with pagination
fastify.get('/api/content', async (request, reply) => {
  const { type, status, limit, skip, q, page, pageSize } = request.query as any;
  
  const take = limit ? parseInt(limit) : (pageSize ? parseInt(pageSize) : 20);
  const skipCount = skip ? parseInt(skip) : (page ? (parseInt(page) - 1) * take : 0);

  const where = {
    ...(type && { type }),
    ...(status && { status }),
    ...(q && {
      title: {
        contains: q,
        mode: 'insensitive' as const
      }
    }),
  };

  const [contents, total] = await Promise.all([
    prisma.content.findMany({
      where,
      take,
      skip: skipCount,
      orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
      include: { genres: true }
    }),
    prisma.content.count({ where })
  ]);

  return { 
    success: true, 
    data: contents, 
    meta: { total, page: Math.floor(skipCount / take) + 1, pageSize: take, totalPages: Math.ceil(total / take) }
  };
});

// News Routes
fastify.get('/api/news', async (request, reply) => {
  const news = await prisma.news.findMany({
    orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
    take: 10
  });
  return { success: true, data: news };
});

// Banner Routes
fastify.get('/api/banners', async (request, reply) => {
  const banners = await prisma.banner.findMany({
    where: { isActive: true },
    orderBy: { order: 'asc' }
  });
  return { success: true, data: banners };
});

// Create News (Admin)
fastify.post('/api/news', async (request, reply) => {
  const data = request.body as any;
  const news = await prisma.news.create({ data });
  return { success: true, data: news };
});

// Genre Route
fastify.get('/api/genres', async (request, reply) => {
  const genres = await prisma.genre.findMany({
    orderBy: { name: 'asc' }
  });
  return { success: true, data: genres };
});

// --- REGISTER ROUTE MODULES ---
fastify.register(contentRoutes);
fastify.register(userRoutes);
fastify.register(taxonomyRoutes);
fastify.register(adminRoutes);
fastify.register(uploadRoutes);
fastify.register(engagementRoutes);
fastify.register(passwordResetRoutes);
fastify.register(scrapeApiRoutes);
fastify.register(permissionRoutes);
fastify.register(analyticsRoutes);
fastify.register(scraperManagementRoutes);
fastify.register(seoRoutes);

// --- SCRAPER MANAGEMENT ROUTES ---

// Get all sources
fastify.get('/api/scrapers', async (request, reply) => {
  const sources = await scraperService.getSources();
  return { success: true, data: sources };
});

// Create new source
fastify.post('/api/scrapers', async (request, reply) => {
  const data = request.body as any;
  const source = await scraperService.createSource(data);
  return { success: true, data: source };
});

// Run scraper (Queue)
fastify.post('/api/scrapers/:id/run', async (request, reply) => {
  const { id } = request.params as { id: string };
  try {
    // Add job to queue
    const job = await scraperQueue.add('scrape', { sourceId: id });
    return { success: true, message: 'Scraping job queued', jobId: job.id };
  } catch (error: any) {
    return reply.status(500).send({ success: false, error: error.message });
  }
});

// Get logs (Updated for new schema)
fastify.get('/api/scrapers/logs', async (request, reply) => {
  const logs = await prisma.scraperLog.findMany({
    include: { source: { select: { name: true, type: true } } },
    orderBy: { startedAt: 'desc' },
    take: 50
  });
  return { success: true, data: logs };
});

// Get Dashboard Stats
fastify.get('/api/scrapers/stats', async (request, reply) => {
  const today = new Date();
  today.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0);

  const [totalSources, activeScrapers, todayLogs] = await Promise.all([
    prisma.scraperSource.count(),
    prisma.scraperLog.count({ where: { status: 'BERJALAN' } }),
    prisma.scraperLog.findMany({ where: { startedAt: { gte: today } } })
  ]);

  let itemsAdded = 0;
  let itemsFailed = 0;
  const running = activeScrapers;
  let successRuns = 0;
  let failedRuns = 0;

  todayLogs.forEach((log: any) => {
    itemsAdded += log.itemsAdded;
    itemsFailed += log.itemsFailed;
    if (log.status === 'SELESAI') successRuns++;
    if (log.status === 'ERROR') failedRuns++;
  });

  return { 
    success: true, 
    data: {
      totalSources,
      activeScrapers,
      todayStats: {
        itemsAdded,
        itemsFailed,
        running,
        successRuns,
        failedRuns,
        totalRuns: todayLogs.length
      }
    } 
  };
});

// Start Server
const start = async () => {
  try {
    const port = parseInt(process.env.PORT || '4000');
    await fastify.listen({ port, host: '0.0.0.0' });
    
    logger.info('Server started successfully', {
      port,
      host: '0.0.0.0',
      environment: process.env.NODE_ENV,
      nodeVersion: process.version,
    });

    // Socket.io setup
    const io = new Server(fastify.server, {
      cors: {
        origin: '*',
      },
    });

    setSocketInstance(io);

    io.on('connection', (socket) => {
      logger.debug('Client connected', {
        socketId: socket.id,
        clientIp: socket.handshake.address,
      });
      
      // Register real-time handlers
      initLiveCommentsHandler(socket, io, prisma);
      initPresenceHandler(io);
      initNotificationHandler(socket, io, prisma);
      
      // Scraper monitoring handlers
      socket.on('scraper:subscribe', (data) => {
        const { sourceId } = data || {};
        if (sourceId) {
          socket.join(`scraper:${sourceId}`);
          logger.debug('Client subscribed to scraper updates', {
            socketId: socket.id,
            sourceId,
          });
        } else {
          socket.join('scraper:all');
          logger.debug('Client subscribed to all scraper updates', {
            socketId: socket.id,
          });
        }
      });

      socket.on('scraper:unsubscribe', (data) => {
        const { sourceId } = data || {};
        if (sourceId) {
          socket.leave(`scraper:${sourceId}`);
          logger.debug('Client unsubscribed from scraper updates', {
            socketId: socket.id,
            sourceId,
          });
        } else {
          socket.leave('scraper:all');
          logger.debug('Client unsubscribed from all scraper updates', {
            socketId: socket.id,
          });
        }
      });
      
      socket.on('disconnect', () => {
        logger.debug('Client disconnected', {
          socketId: socket.id,
        });
      });

      socket.on('error', (error) => {
        logger.error('Socket error', error, {
          socketId: socket.id,
        });
      });
    });

    // Initialize BullMQ Worker with Socket.io
    initScraperWorker(io);

    logger.info(`Gateway running at http://localhost:${port}`);
  } catch (err) {
    logger.fatal('Failed to start server', err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(String(err)), {
      port: process.env.PORT || '4000',
    });
    process.exit(1);
  }
};

// Handle uncaught exceptions
process.on('uncaughtException', (error) => {
  logger.fatal('Uncaught exception', error);
  process.exit(1);
});

// Handle unhandled promise rejections
process.on('unhandledRejection', (reason, promise) => {
  logger.fatal('Unhandled rejection', new Error(String(reason)), {
    promise: String(promise),
  });
  process.exit(1);
});

// Graceful shutdown
process.on('SIGTERM', async () => {
  logger.info('SIGTERM received, shutting down gracefully');
  await fastify.close();
  await logger.close();
  process.exit(0);
});

process.on('SIGINT', async () => {
  logger.info('SIGINT received, shutting down gracefully');
  await fastify.close();
  await logger.close();
  process.exit(0);
});

start();

</content>

**Trigger**: When queue state changes (enqueue/clear)  
**Methods**: `ScraperQueueService.enqueueJob()`, `ScraperQueueService.clearAll()`

### Socket.io Event Tests
All Socket.io events are verified in the test suite:
- ✅ Job started events
- ✅ Job progress events
- ✅ Job completed events
- ✅ Job failed events
- ✅ Queue updated events

---

## Client Integration Guide

### Basic Connection
```typescript
import io from 'socket.io-client';

const socket = io('http://localhost:4000');

// Subscribe to all scraper events
socket.emit('scraper:subscribe', {});

// Listen for events
socket.on('scraper:job:started', (data) => {
  console.log('Job started:', data.job.id);
});

socket.on('scraper:job:progress', (data) => {
  console.log('Progress:', data.progress.percentage + '%');
});

socket.on('scraper:job:completed', (data) => {
  console.log('Job completed:', data.job.id);
});

socket.on('scraper:job:failed', (data) => {
  console.log('Job failed:', data.job.id, data.job.error);
});

socket.on('scraper:queue:updated', (data) => {
  console.log('Queue updated:', data.queueStats);
});
```

### React Hook
```typescript
import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import io from 'socket.io-client';

export function useScraperMonitoring(sourceId?: string) {
  const [socket, setSocket] = useState(null);
  const [queueStats, setQueueStats] = useState(null);
  const [connected, setConnected] = useState(false);

  useEffect(() => {
    const newSocket = io('http://localhost:4000');
    setSocket(newSocket);

    newSocket.on('connect', () => {
      setConnected(true);
      newSocket.emit('scraper:subscribe', sourceId ? { sourceId } : {});
    });

    newSocket.on('scraper:queue:updated', (data) => {
      setQueueStats(data.queueStats);
    });

    // ... other event handlers

    return () => {
      newSocket.emit('scraper:unsubscribe', sourceId ? { sourceId } : {});
      newSocket.close();
    };
  }, [sourceId]);

  return { socket, connected, queueStats };
}
```

---

## API Endpoints Integration

The following API endpoints trigger Socket.io events:

### POST /api/admin/scrapers/:id/run
- **Triggers**: `scraper:queue:updated` (enqueued)
- **Then**: `scraper:job:started` → `scraper:job:progress` → `scraper:job:completed` or `scraper:job:failed`

### POST /api/admin/scrapers/queue/clear
- **Triggers**: `scraper:queue:updated` (cleared)

### POST /api/admin/scrapers/jobs/:jobId/cancel
- **Triggers**: `scraper:job:failed` (cancelled)

### POST /api/admin/scrapers/jobs/:jobId/retry
- **Triggers**: `scraper:queue:updated` (enqueued)

---

## Error Handling

### Graceful Degradation
If Socket.io is not available, the system continues to work:
```typescript
const io = getSocketInstance();
if (io) {
  io.emit('scraper:job:started', data);
}
// No error thrown if io is null
```

### Logging
All Socket.io operations are logged:
- Connection/disconnection events
- Subscribe/unsubscribe events
- Event emissions
- Errors

### Event Frequency
- **Job Started**: Once per job
- **Job Progress**: Configurable (recommended: max 1/second)
- **Job Completed**: Once per job
- **Job Failed**: Once per job (or per retry)
- **Queue Updated**: On enqueue/clear operations

### Room-based Broadcasting
- Clients can subscribe to specific sources: `scraper:{sourceId}`
- Or all scrapers: `scraper:all`
- Reduces unnecessary event traffic

### Payload Size
- Event payloads are kept minimal
- Only essential data is included
- Large result data is not sent via Socket.io

---

## Security Considerations

### Current Implementation
- ✅ CORS enabled (currently `*` for development)
- ✅ Error handling
- ✅ Logging
- ⚠️ No authentication (should be added for production)

3. **Rate Limiting**: Limit Socket.io connections per IP
4. **Input Validation**: Validate all incoming Socket.io events

# Scraper Health Check Implementation Summary

**Date**: May 18, 2026  
**Status**: ✅ Completed  
**Test Coverage**: 19 tests passing

---

## 📋 Overview

Implemented comprehensive health check system for scraper sources with periodic monitoring, status tracking, and real-time notifications.

---

## 🎯 Features Implemented

### 1. Health Check Service (`scraperHealthCheckService.ts`)

#### Core Functionality
- **Single Source Health Check**: Check individual scraper source availability
- **Bulk Health Check**: Check all enabled sources simultaneously
- **Automatic Periodic Checks**: Schedule automated health monitoring
- **Health Status Tracking**: Track consecutive failures and success rates
- **Real-time Notifications**: Socket.io events for health status changes

#### Health Status Levels
- `HEALTHY`: Source is accessible and performing well
- `DEGRADED`: Source is accessible but slow or experiencing issues
- `UNHEALTHY`: Source is inaccessible or failing consistently
- `UNKNOWN`: Health status not yet determined

#### Health Check Metrics
- Response time measurement
- HTTP status code tracking
- Consecutive failure counting
- Last successful check timestamp
- Performance threshold monitoring

### 2. API Endpoints

#### Manual Health Checks
```
POST   /api/admin/scrapers/:id/health-check
       - Manually trigger health check for a source
       - Returns: HealthCheckResult with status, response time, errors

POST   /api/admin/scrapers/health-check/all
       - Trigger health check for all enabled sources
       - Returns: Summary and individual results
```

#### Health Status Queries
```
GET    /api/admin/scrapers/:id/health
       - Get current health status for a source
       - Returns: Health history with consecutive failures

GET    /api/admin/scrapers/health/statistics
       - Get aggregated health statistics
       - Returns: Total, healthy, degraded, unhealthy counts
```

#### Automatic Health Checks
```
POST   /api/admin/scrapers/:id/health-check/auto/start
       - Start automatic health checks for a source
       - Configurable check interval

POST   /api/admin/scrapers/:id/health-check/auto/stop
       - Stop automatic health checks for a source

POST   /api/admin/scrapers/health-check/auto/start-global
       - Start automatic health checks for all enabled sources
       - Configurable check interval

POST   /api/admin/scrapers/health-check/auto/stop-global
       - Stop all automatic health checks
```

#### Health History Management
```
POST   /api/admin/scrapers/:id/health/reset
       - Reset health history for a source
       - Clears consecutive failure count
```

### 3. Configuration Options

```typescript
interface HealthCheckConfig {
  timeout: number;                    // Default: 10000ms (10 seconds)
  performanceThreshold: number;       // Default: 3000ms (3 seconds)
  degradedThreshold: number;          // Default: 2 consecutive failures
  unhealthyThreshold: number;         // Default: 5 consecutive failures
  checkInterval: number;              // Default: 300000ms (5 minutes)
}
```

### 4. Integration Points

#### With ScraperSourceService
- Uses `testSource()` method for connectivity checks
- Updates source statistics on health check results
- Retrieves enabled sources for bulk checks

#### With Error Recovery Service
- Leverages error classification for intelligent retry logic
- Uses circuit breaker patterns for failing sources
- Applies exponential backoff for degraded sources

#### With Socket.io
- Emits `scraper:health:checked` event on individual checks
- Emits `scraper:health:summary` event on bulk checks
- Real-time updates to admin dashboard

#### With Database
- Stores health status in ScraperSource.config
- Updates source status field based on health
- Tracks health check history

---

## 🔧 Technical Implementation

### Health Check Algorithm

1. **Connectivity Test**
   - Performs HTTP HEAD request to source URL
   - Applies configurable timeout
   - Uses custom headers if specified

2. **Status Evaluation**
   - Checks HTTP status code (2xx/3xx = valid)
   - Measures response time
   - Compares against performance threshold

3. **Health Classification**
   - `HEALTHY`: Valid response + good performance
   - `DEGRADED`: Valid response + slow performance
   - `UNHEALTHY`: Invalid response or connection failure

4. **History Tracking**
   - Increments consecutive failures on error
   - Resets counter on success
   - Adjusts status based on failure threshold

5. **Database Update**
   - Stores health status in source config
   - Updates source status field
   - Logs health check results

### Automatic Monitoring

#### Per-Source Monitoring
- Individual interval timers per source
- Independent configuration per source
- Can be started/stopped individually

#### Global Monitoring
- Single interval for all enabled sources
- Checks all sources in parallel
- Provides aggregated statistics

---

## 📊 Health Check Results

### HealthCheckResult Interface
```typescript
{
  sourceId: string;
  sourceName: string;
  status: HealthStatus;
  responseTime?: number;
  statusCode?: number;
  error?: string;
  checkedAt: Date;
  consecutiveFailures: number;
  lastSuccessfulCheck?: Date;
  details: {
    isAccessible: boolean;
    hasValidResponse: boolean;
    meetsPerformanceThreshold: boolean;
  }
}
```

- ✅ Clears all health history

#### Statistics Tests
- ✅ Gets health statistics

#### Edge Cases
- ✅ Handles source not found
- ✅ Handles empty enabled sources list
- ✅ Prevents duplicate global auto check

---

## 🔄 Socket.io Events

### Events Emitted

#### Individual Health Check
```typescript
io.emit('scraper:health:checked', {
  result: HealthCheckResult,
  timestamp: Date
});
```

#### Bulk Health Check Summary
```typescript
io.emit('scraper:health:summary', {
  summary: {
    total: number,
    healthy: number,
    degraded: number,
    unhealthy: number
  },
  results: HealthCheckResult[],
  timestamp: Date
});
```

---

## 📝 Usage Examples

### Manual Health Check
```typescript
// Check single source
const result = await scraperHealthCheckService.checkSourceHealth('source-id');
console.log(`Status: ${result.status}, Response Time: ${result.responseTime}ms`);

// Check all sources

console.log(`Checked ${results.length} sources`);
```

### Automatic Monitoring
```typescript

  checkInterval: 300000,
  performanceThreshold: 3000
});

// Start global monitoring

  checkInterval: 300000

// Stop monitoring

```

### Get Health Statistics
```typescript

console.log(`Total: ${stats.total}, Healthy: ${stats.healthy}`);
```

---

## 🔐 Security & Permissions

- All endpoints require admin authentication
- Uses `requireAdmin` middleware

# Proxy Rotation Service

## Overview

The Proxy Rotation Service provides comprehensive proxy pool management for scraper sources, including automatic rotation, health tracking, and failover capabilities. This service helps avoid IP blocking by rotating through multiple proxy servers when making scraper requests.

## Features

### 1. Proxy Pool Management
- Add, remove, and update proxies dynamically
- Support for multiple proxy types (HTTP, HTTPS, SOCKS5)
- Priority-based proxy selection
- Automatic proxy pool initialization per scraper source

### 2. Health Tracking
- Real-time health monitoring for each proxy
- Automatic health checks at configurable intervals
- Track success/failure rates and response times
- Consecutive failure tracking

### 3. Automatic Failover
- Auto-disable proxies after consecutive failures
- Automatic re-enable after cooldown period
- Circuit breaker pattern for failed proxies
- Graceful degradation when proxies fail

### 4. Selection Strategies
- **Round Robin**: Rotate through proxies in order
- **Random**: Select random proxy from pool
- **Least Used**: Select proxy with fewest requests
- **Best Performance**: Select proxy with best response time
- **Priority**: Select by priority (highest first)

### 5. Performance Metrics
- Average response time per proxy
- Success/failure rates
- Total requests per proxy
- Pool-wide statistics

## Configuration

### Proxy Configuration
- **url**: Proxy URL (format: `protocol://[user:pass@]host:port`)
- **type**: Proxy type (HTTP, HTTPS, SOCKS5)
- **status**: Proxy status (ACTIVE, DISABLED, FAILED, TESTING)
- **priority**: Selection priority (higher = preferred)
- **maxFailures**: Max consecutive failures before auto-disable
- **healthCheckInterval**: Time between health checks (ms)
- **timeout**: Request timeout (ms)

### Pool Configuration
- **selectionStrategy**: How to select next proxy
- **healthCheckEnabled**: Enable automatic health checks
- **healthCheckInterval**: Default health check interval (ms)
- **autoDisableOnFailure**: Auto-disable after max failures
- **maxConsecutiveFailures**: Threshold for auto-disable
- **reEnableAfter**: Cooldown before re-enabling (ms)

## Health Metrics

Each proxy tracks the following metrics:

- **isHealthy**: Current health status
- **consecutiveFailures**: Current failure streak
- **consecutiveSuccesses**: Current success streak
- **totalRequests**: Total requests made
- **successfulRequests**: Successful requests
- **failedRequests**: Failed requests
- **averageResponseTime**: Average response time (ms)
- **lastUsed**: Last request timestamp
- **lastSuccess**: Last successful request
- **lastFailure**: Last failed request
- **lastError**: Last error message
- **lastHealthCheck**: Last health check timestamp

## Best Practices

### 1. Proxy Selection
- Use **PRIORITY** strategy for known reliable proxies
- Use **BEST_PERFORMANCE** for optimal speed
- Use **ROUND_ROBIN** for even distribution
- Use **LEAST_USED** to balance load

### 2. Health Checks
- Enable health checks for production
- Set interval based on proxy reliability (5-10 minutes)
- Monitor health metrics regularly

### 3. Failure Handling
- Set `maxConsecutiveFailures` to 3-5
- Set `reEnableAfter` to 10-30 minutes
- Enable `autoDisableOnFailure` for automatic recovery

### 4. Performance
- Use multiple proxies (3-5 minimum)
- Set appropriate timeouts (20-30 seconds)
- Monitor average response times
- Remove consistently slow proxies

### 5. Security
- Use authenticated proxies when possible
- Rotate proxy credentials regularly
- Monitor for suspicious activity
- Use HTTPS/SOCKS5 for sensitive data

## Troubleshooting

### All Proxies Disabled
**Cause**: All proxies failed health checks or exceeded max failures

**Solution**:
1. Check proxy connectivity manually
2. Verify proxy credentials
3. Check if target site is blocking proxies
4. Manually enable proxies: `POST /api/admin/proxies/:id/enable`
5. Adjust `maxConsecutiveFailures` threshold

### Slow Response Times
**Cause**: Proxies are slow or overloaded

**Solution**:
1. Check average response times: `GET /api/admin/proxies/health/all`
2. Remove slow proxies
3. Add faster proxies
4. Use `BEST_PERFORMANCE` selection strategy

### Frequent Failures
**Cause**: Proxies are unreliable or target site is blocking

**Solution**:
1. Check failure patterns in health metrics
2. Test proxies manually: `POST /api/admin/proxies/:id/test`
3. Verify target site isn't blocking proxy IPs
4. Rotate to different proxy providers
5. Increase retry count in scraper config

### Proxy Not Being Used
**Cause**: Proxy is disabled or unhealthy

**Solution**:
1. Check proxy status: `GET /api/admin/proxies/:id`
2. Enable if disabled: `POST /api/admin/proxies/:id/enable`
3. Check health metrics
4. Run manual test: `POST /api/admin/proxies/:id/test`

## Technical Architecture

### Proxy Pool Structure

```typescript
Map<proxyId, ProxyConfig> {
  id: string,
  url: string,
  type: ProxyType,
  status: ProxyStatus,
  priority: number,
  maxFailures: number,
  healthCheckInterval: number,
  timeout: number,
  createdAt: Date,
  updatedAt: Date
}
```

### Health Tracking Structure

```typescript
Map<proxyId, ProxyHealth> {
  proxyId: string,
  isHealthy: boolean,
  consecutiveFailures: number,
  consecutiveSuccesses: number,
  totalRequests: number,
  successfulRequests: number,
  failedRequests: number,
  averageResponseTime: number,
  lastUsed?: Date,
  lastSuccess?: Date,
  lastFailure?: Date,
  lastError?: string,
  lastHealthCheck?: Date
}
```

### Selection Strategies

1. **Round Robin**: Cycles through proxies in order
2. **Random**: Randomly selects from available proxies
3. **Least Used**: Selects proxy with fewest total requests
4. **Best Performance**: Selects proxy with lowest average response time
5. **Priority**: Selects proxy with highest priority value

### Auto-Failover Logic

```
Request Fails
    ↓
Increment consecutiveFailures
    ↓
Check: consecutiveFailures >= maxConsecutiveFailures?
    ↓ Yes
Disable Proxy
    ↓
Schedule Re-enable (after reEnableAfter ms)
    ↓
Re-enable Proxy (reset failure count)
```
